items.headline: City affordability protections for subsidized housing discussed
items.summary: Council reviewed policies to maintain affordable housing restrictions if project subsidies expire or properties face foreclosure. This affects long-term affordability of assisted housing developments across San José.

items.headline: Storm sewer system master plan expansion approved
items.summary: City advanced a city-wide master planning effort for the storm sewer system through a contract amendment with Wood Rodgers, Inc. This addresses aging infrastructure and stormwater management across San José.

items.headline: City Council reviews $2026-27 capital improvement spending plan
items.summary: Mountain View is examining its major infrastructure and facilities projects for the coming fiscal year. This study session determines where the city will invest in roads, parks, utilities, and public buildings over the next 12 months.

items.headline: Sunnyvale updates tenant protection rules in new ordinance
items.summary: The city is amending its residential tenant protections program, which could affect housing rights and landlord-tenant policies. Details on what changes are proposed were not provided in the agenda.

items.headline: Library facilities and customer experience plan presented
items.summary: Council received a status report on the Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan. This indicates the city is evaluating library services and physical facility improvements for residents.

items.headline: Historic downtown building eligible for major expansion
items.summary: The building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown Palo Alto is being considered for elevation from a Category 4 to Category 2 historic resource, which would allow an additional 5,000 square feet of floor area as an incentive for seismic and historic rehabilitation. This could substantially expand a downtown structure while preserving its historic character.

items.headline: City of Cupertino launches summer jobs program for youth
items.summary: The city presented a new Summer Jobs program aimed at employing local young people. This could be relevant for residents with teenagers looking for summer work opportunities.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed for San Antonio Road
items.summary: A proposal to rezone and redevelop two properties at 788-790 and 796 San Antonio Road into an eight-story building with 167 residential units (28 affordable) and 1,400 square feet of commercial space is under city review. This represents significant new housing and a notable change to that commercial corridor.
